introSection: Skyliner is a wooden roller coaster originally built in 1960 for Roseland Park in Canandaigua, New York, but relocated to Lakemont Park in Altoona, Pennsylvania after Roseland's closure in 1985. Skyliner was designed and built by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company. As of 2024, the ride is standing but not operating.

sections.title: Relocation
sections.content: In 1987, Skyliner opened at its new home in Lakemont Park after having been reconstructed following the move from Roseland Park. During the reconstruction process, several pieces needed to be replaced due to wear and tear, as well as termite damage. However, the ride still retained the original track layout and elements.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Skyliner is best known for the presence of several non-banked 90 and 180 degree turns that roughly force riders to the sides of the cars.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Each car features single position lap bars.

fullText:
Skyliner is a wooden roller coaster originally built in 1960 for Roseland Park in Canandaigua, New York, but relocated to Lakemont Park in Altoona, Pennsylvania after Roseland's closure in 1985. Skyliner was designed and built by the Philadelphia Toboggan Company. As of 2024, the ride is standing but not operating.

In 1987, Skyliner opened at its new home in Lakemont Park after having been reconstructed following the move from Roseland Park. During the reconstruction process, several pieces needed to be replaced due to wear and tear, as well as termite damage. However, the ride still retained the original track layout and elements.

Skyliner is best known for the presence of several non-banked 90 and 180 degree turns that roughly force riders to the sides of the cars.

Each car features single position lap bars.
